items.summary: The Lost Boys found new life on Broadway with the musical adaptation of the 1987 film, now playing at the Palace Theatre. items.title: ‘Joe Turner’s Come and Gone’ Broadway Review: Taraji P. Henson and Cedric the Entertainer Lead  Stellar Cast
items.summary: Nearly 40 years after its debut, “Joe Turner’s Come and Gone” has returned to Broadway. The second installment in legendary playwright August Wilson’s “The Century Cycle,” “Joe Turner’s Come and Gone, grapples with Black life in the decades after Emancipation. Directed by Debbie Allen, the emotionally gripping play is set in a Pittsburgh boarding house. […]

Allen saw the Broadway premiere of the play on this stage in 1988. She returns now with Taraji P. Henson, Cedric the Entertainer, Joshua Boone and Ruben Santiago-Hudson. items.title: Jessica Vosk Talks Career Risks, Resilience and Originating Her First Role in Beaches
items.summary: Jessica Vosk is no stranger to Broadway, but her star turn as Cee Cee Bloom in Beaches represents a meaningful first in a career already filled with standout performances. In conversation with The Broadway Show host Tamsen Fadal, Vosk reflects on the unpredictable rhythm of life between productions, the rare opportunity to originate a role and the personal journey that brought her to this moment.

items.summary: He is stepping in for current actor Eric Anderson as Anderson returns to the role of Zidler in “Moulin Rouge! The Musical.”

items.title: ‘Beaches’ partners with Lady Gaga’s Born This Way Foundation
items.summary: The Broadway musical and the nonprofit will launch a kindness and mental health initiative.

items.title: Raúl Esparza and Helen J Shen will announce 2026 Drama Desk Award nominations
items.summary: Nominations will be announced on April 29.

items.title: Criminal justice reform advocate Jason Flom joins ‘Fear of 13’ producing team
items.summary: Lindsey Ferrentino’s new play is based on the real-life wrongful conviction of Nick Yarris.

items.title: How co-directors Zhailon Levingston and Bill Rauch merged musical theater and ballroom to create a ‘third event’ with ‘Cats’
items.summary: The artists say that the Broadway mounting of “Cats: The Jellicle Ball” is the deeper expression of their goal to merge forms.

items.title: Grosses analysis: Broadway numbers take a dip, despite three new shows opening
items.summary: For the week ending April 19, the 40 running productions grossed $39,084,851.

Broadway Pipeline: April 22, 2026
Outer Critics Circle announces nominees for 2026
items.summary: Broadway’s “Lost Boys” leads the pack with 11 nominations; “Death of a Salesman” is the most-nominated Broadway play.

items.title: ‘The Balusters’ Broadway Review: Anika Noni Rose, Richard Thomas Star in Brilliant and Brutally Funny Look at a Dysfunctional Neighborhood Association
items.summary: It’s easy to get depressed about national politics. The people running things are nasty, brutish and eager to appeal to baser instincts rather than better angels. As “The Balusters,” the story of a neighborhood association riven by prejudice and petty skirmishes, demonstrates, politics aren’t much better at the local level. The debate here may be […]

items.title: ‘Schmigadoon!’ Broadway Review: TV Series Adaptation Delivers a Fizzy and Delightful Love Letter to Musical Theater
items.summary: It’s tempting to argue on principle that “Schmigadoon!” is everything that’s wrong with Broadway: a double-baked potato of familiar IP that relies on affection for a TV series, which itself relies on affection for golden age musicals. But the effervescent stage show, from creator Cinco Paul and director-choreographer Christoper Gattelli, is all but irresistible — […]

items.title: ‘Fallen Angels’ Broadway Review: Rose Byrne and Kelli O’Hara Sparkle in Lackluster Noël Coward Revival
items.summary: There’s a bit of acting advice that’s often ascribed to Noël Coward: “Speak clearly, and don’t bump into the furniture.” But if you’re Kelli O’Hara and Rose Byrne, by all means, slide down the staircase, nosedive over the settee and slur your words while tossing back two strong martinis and a case of Dom Pérignon. […]

items.title: School Employee Charged After Students Discover Hidden Camera in Theater Area
items.summary: Police in Maryland say a High School theater staff member secretly recorded underage students in a theater changing room.

items.title: ‘Proof’ Broadway Review: Ayo Edebiri and Don Cheadle Lead a Gripping but Oddly Paced Revival
items.summary: Grief is one of the most confounding aspects of the human experience. To live is to experience loss, and yet, we are never truly prepared. This type of agony is always a detriment to mental health, even more so when someone is already predisposed to instability. In the first Broadway revival of Pulitzer Prize-winning playwright […]

items.title: Comedian Cat Cohen’s One-Woman Show ‘Broad Strokes’ Sets Off-Broadway Debut (EXCLUSIVE)
items.summary: Comedian Cat Cohen’s new one-woman show “Broad Strokes” will debut Off-Broadway over the summer. The musical comedy is set to begin previews at downtown Manhattan’s Lucille Lortel Theatre on July 14 ahead of opening night on July 27. The eight-week engagement will end on Sept. 5. “Broad Strokes” heads to New York following a sold-out […]

items.title: ‘A Walk on the Moon,’ Musical Adaptation of 1999 Movie, Sets New York Stage Debut (EXCLUSIVE)
items.summary: “A Walk on the Moon,” a musical about a Brooklyn mother whose life is transformed during a trip to a Catskills bungalow colony, will have its New York premiere this summer at The Laura Pels Theatre. The story is familiar to fans of the 1999 romantic drama of the same name that starred Diane Lane […]

items.title: Australian Theatre Events Canceled After Hidden Cameras Allegedly Found in Change Rooms
items.summary: A police investigation is underway in Australia after hidden cameras were allegedly found in change rooms at Horsham Town Hall, leading to canceled performances, venue closures, and growing concern across the local theatre community.

items.title: Book of Mormon Performance Halted in London After Audience Member Disrupts Show
items.summary: A West End performance of The Book of Mormon was canceled mid-show after a protester allegedly threw objects at the cast.

items.title: Congo Square Theatre Ensemble says board conflict, financial questions led to the collapse of historic company
items.summary: The ensemble at Congo Square Theatre Company is speaking out about what it says led to the collapse of the historic Chicago theatre organization.

items.title: The Addams Family Musical Goes Bilingual!
items.summary: Bilingual Broadway Collective (BBCo) announces its first educational production: The Addams Family, performed in English and Spanish.

items.title: Former Gettysburg Mayor and Theatre Leader Charged in Child Sex Crime Case
items.summary: Former Gettysburg Mayor Chad-Alan Carr, who also had ties to local theatre and school productions, has been charged in a child sex crime case.

items.title: Video Shows Children Threatened With ICE Outside Chicago Dance Studio
items.summary: A group of children leaving a Mexican dance studio in Chicago were met with slurs, liquid thrown at them, and threats about ICE.

items.title: Production of “Jesus Christ Superstar” Canceled After Cast Walkout, Prompting Safety Concerns
items.summary: More than half the cast of a “Jesus Christ Superstar” production walked out, leading to the show’s cancellation. The controversy is raising larger questions about safety, leadership, and trust in theatre.

items.title: New Stage Rules Put Virginia School Theatre Programs in a Tough Spot
items.summary: I’m all for safety in school theatre. But at what point does “safety” become overreach? The situation unfolding in Virginia feels less like thoughtful protection and more like bureaucracy making student theatre harder to do.

items.title: Parents Demand Answers After Drama Teacher Sentenced
items.summary: A drama teacher has been sentenced in a sexual communication case involving a child. Now parents are asking the question that matters most: what safeguards failed, and what changes happen now to protect students?

items.title: Theater Company Cancels Production After Epstein Files Mention Playwright
items.summary: A Rhode Island theatre has canceled its upcoming production of Lauren Gunderson’s The Revolutionists after her name appeared in the latest Epstein files. Gunderson says she never knew or met Epstein and that his email was included on a contact list she was given, without her awareness.

items.title: Colorado High School Pulls Sponsorship of “Shrek” After Pride Flags Used in Show
items.summary: A Colorado high school pulled its sponsorship of a community theatre production of Shrek the Musical after Pride flags were used during “Freak Flag.”

items.title: Cast Members Resign from Chicago Production Amid Misconduct Allegations Against Director
items.summary: Multiple cast members have resigned from Invictus Theatre Company’s Cat on a Hot Tin Roof mid-rehearsal after allegations of sexual and power harassment and targeted bullying were raised against the director and artistic director, Charles Askenaizer.

items.title: Questions Swirling After Texas Theatre Teachers Are Placed on Administrative Leave
items.summary: Two Theatre teachers in Texas have been placed on administrative leave as questions continue and details remain limited.

items.title: Video Shows Man Confronting Audience Members During Broadway’s “Mamma Mia!” Over Noise
items.summary: A video from Mamma Mia! shows how quickly a night at the theatre can go sideways. Singing too loud isn’t okay. Losing your mind over it isn’t either.

items.title: Theater Teacher Faces Multiple Sexual Assault Charges as Questions Mount Over Prior Complaints
items.summary: A Las Vegas theater teacher is facing multiple sexual assault charges, with former students describing years of troubling behavior.

items.title: Kennedy Center Threatens to Sue Artist Over Protest Cancellation
items.summary: A jazz musician canceled a show on principle. The Kennedy Center responded with a lawsuit threat. That contrast tells you everything you need to know.

items.title: Imani Dia Smith, Former Child Star of Broadway’s “The Lion King”, Dies at 25
items.summary: The theatre community is mourning the loss of Imani Dia Smith, a former Lion King child performer remembered for her talent, generosity, and spirit. She was 25.

items.title: Actor Fired From “A Christmas Carol” After Injury Forces Missed Rehearsal
items.summary: An actor was fired from a production of A Christmas Carol after missing a rehearsal due to an injury. The injury was reportedly sustained during the production itself.
